message:
fix(mux-pool,wezterm): skip retry and circuit breaker for cancelled mux operations

Cancelled operations are a caller decision, not a connection health
signal. They must not trigger recovery retries (which would waste
resources reconnecting for an operation the caller already abandoned)
or trip the circuit breaker (which would penalize healthy connections).

mux_pool.rs (execute_with_recovery + execute_with_recovery_with_cx):
- Check err.is_cancelled() before evaluating retry eligibility. If
  cancelled, can_retry is forced to false regardless of recovery config
  and attempt count. The cancelled state is logged in tracing spans
  for observability.
- New test: pool_execute_with_recovery_with_cx_does_not_retry_cancelled_mux_io
  seeds a real client, injects a synthetic cancellation error, and
  asserts: zero recovery_attempts, zero connections_created, exactly
  one acquire (the initial one that got the error).

wezterm.rs (mux_error_is_circuit_breaker_trigger):
- Extend the Mux(DirectMuxError) arm to also exclude errors where
  is_cancelled() is true. Previously only Pool(Cancelled) was excluded,
  but a transport-level cancellation (Mux(Io(Interrupted+"cancelled")))
  was still treated as a circuit breaker trigger.
- New test: mux_transport_cancellation_does_not_trigger_circuit_breaker
  constructs the transport-level cancellation variant and asserts it
  does not trigger the circuit breaker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
